lead: A home removals survey is the step that turns a rough idea into an accurate, honest quote, yet many people are unsure what it actually involves. There is nothing to dread. In our experience it is simply a friendly, practical walk around your home so we can see what is involved and plan the move properly. As a family-run firm based in Westerfield near Ipswich, we have surveyed homes of every shape across Suffolk since 2005. Here is exactly how a survey works and how to get the most from it.

sections.h: Why a Survey Comes Before Any Quote
sections.body:
A trustworthy quote starts with a survey, never a number plucked from the air over the phone. The reason is simple: no two homes are the same, and the only way to price a move with confidence is to see it. A survey lets us judge the true volume of your belongings, look closely at access at both ends, and spot the things that shape the day. Without that, any figure is a guess, and guesses tend to fall apart on the morning of the move when reality turns out larger or trickier than imagined.

We have found that a proper survey protects you as much as it protects us. It means the quote you receive is one we can stand behind, with no nasty surprises and no quiet additions later. It also gives you the chance to ask questions, raise worries and tell us about anything unusual while there is still plenty of time to plan. A survey is really a planning conversation as much as a measuring exercise, and the time it takes is repaid many times over on moving day.

sections.h: In-Person or Video: Choosing the Right Survey
sections.body:
There are two ways we carry out a survey, and both work well. An in-person survey is the traditional approach, where a member of our team comes to your home, walks through each room with you, and takes in the whole picture first-hand. Many customers prefer this because it feels reassuring to meet the firm face to face, ask questions on the spot, and point out the items they are most concerned about. It usually takes somewhere between twenty minutes and an hour depending on the size of the home.

A video survey is the modern alternative, and it has become genuinely popular. Using a phone or tablet, you walk us through your home on a live video call while we ask questions and make notes, just as we would in person. It is quick to arrange, easy to fit around a busy week, and ideal if you are moving from a distance or simply prefer not to have a visitor. The accuracy is excellent provided you take your time and show us everything, including the spaces that are easy to skip past.

Whichever you choose, the goal is identical: a clear, complete picture of your move. We are happy to recommend one over the other based on your circumstances, but neither is a lesser option. The right survey is simply the one that lets us see your home properly, and we will always guide you on getting the best result from whichever you pick.

sections.h: What We Look At Room by Room
sections.body:
During the survey we work through your home methodically, room by room, building a picture of the volume and the contents. We are looking at the furniture, the boxes you will fill, and the larger items that need particular thought. We will note wardrobes and beds that may need dismantling, white goods that will need disconnecting, and anything heavy or bulky that calls for extra hands. None of this is intrusive; we simply need to understand what is going on the lorry so we can bring the right van and the right crew.

Crucially, we always ask to see the spaces people forget. The loft, the garage, the shed, the conservatory and the cupboard under the stairs are where hidden volume hides, and they are exactly the areas a hurried self-estimate overlooks. We have learned over the years to ask gently but firmly about these, because the difference between a tidy living room and a packed garage can change the size of the job considerably. Showing us everything, even the bits you are slightly embarrassed about, gives you the most accurate quote.

We will also talk through what you intend to take and what you plan to leave or let go. A survey is a fine moment to mention that you are decluttering, selling some furniture, or leaving the white goods behind, because all of that changes the volume. The clearer you are about the final contents, the tighter the quote, so do tell us if the home we are looking at will look noticeably lighter by moving day.

Access is one of the biggest things a survey checks, because it affects how long the move takes, and time is the heart of any quote. At your current home we look at where the lorry can park, how far our crews will carry to reach it, whether there are stairs, and how wide the doorways and hallways are. We note narrow lanes, gated entrances, parking restrictions and anything that might slow loading down. A tight Suffolk village lane or a town-centre permit zone is far better known about now than discovered on the day.

We assess the destination just as carefully, even though you may not yet live there, because a move is only as smooth as its harder end. If you can tell us about the new property's parking, stairs and doorways, or share photos and details from the estate agent, we will factor it all in. A ground-floor pickup means little if the new place is up two flights with a long carry, so we always plan around the more demanding of the two addresses to keep the quote realistic.

sections.h: Talking Through Packing, Timing and Storage
sections.body:
A survey is also the natural moment to settle the practical choices. We will ask how much packing you would like to do yourself and how much you would like to hand to us, from a full professional pack to simply loading boxes you have filled. We can talk through supplying boxes and materials, dismantling and reassembling furniture, and handling appliances. There is no pressure either way; we just want the quote to reflect the service you genuinely want, so you are paying for the right level of help and nothing you do not need.

We will discuss dates and any flexibility you have, since the end of the month, weekends and the spring and summer are the busiest times. And we will raise storage if there is any chance of a gap between moving out and moving in. Being family-run with our own secure storage here in Westerfield, we can fold a few days or weeks into the plan easily, which beats arranging it in a hurry if a completion date slips. By the end of the survey we should have a shared, honest plan for the whole move.

Once the survey is done, we put together your quote in writing. A written quote matters because it sets out clearly what is included and what is not, so there is no ambiguity later. We will detail the crew, the vehicle, the services you have chosen and any extras, and we will confirm that your move is fully covered, which is something you should always check with anyone before booking. You are under no obligation at this point; the quote is yours to consider in your own time.

We always encourage customers to read the quote carefully and come back with questions. If something is unclear, ask. If your plans change, perhaps you declutter further, the date shifts, or the destination firms up, tell us and we will revise the figure rather than leave you guessing. A survey is not a sales trap; it is the foundation of a smooth move. When you are ready to book, the plan we built together at the survey becomes the plan our crews follow on the day, which is precisely how a move should run.

faqs: How long does a home removals survey take?

Usually between twenty minutes and an hour, depending on the size of your home and how much there is to see. A larger, busier property naturally takes a little longer, while a small flat can be assessed quite quickly. We never rush, because an accurate picture is what makes the quote reliable.
Is a video survey as accurate as an in-person one?

Yes, provided you take your time and show us everything, including the loft, garage and shed. A video survey lets you walk us through your home on a live call while we ask questions and make notes, just as we would in person. It is quick to arrange and works well, especially over a distance.
Do I have to pay for a survey or quote?

No. Our surveys and quotes are free and come with no obligation. The survey is simply how we build an accurate figure we can stand behind, and you are free to take your time considering it before deciding whether to book.
What should I show the surveyor?

Show us everything that is moving, especially the spaces people forget such as the loft, garage, shed and cupboard under the stairs. Mention any large, heavy or fragile items, anything you plan to declutter, and any access or parking issues at both ends. The fuller the picture, the more accurate your quote.
